title: Add read-only volume mount to play kube
body:
add support for read-only volume mounts in podman play kube

Closes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7550

Signed-off-by: Ashley Cui <acui@redhat.com>

comments.body: LGTM once comment from @rhatdan is addressed

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *ashley-cui*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@ashley-cui could you make sure that `podman generate kube` works correctly also?  IE If a container has a readonly volume mounted in it, when I generate the yaml file, it ends up with the correct flag.
